steps.text: Click Open Console to launch the AWS Management Console in a new browser tab.
On the login page: Retain the default Account ID (12-digit number; do not edit). Input the provided IAM Username and Password. Click Sign In.
Retain the default Account ID (12-digit number; do not edit).
Input the provided IAM Username and Password.
Click Sign In.
In the Console, select us-east-1 (N. Virginia) from the top-right region dropdown.
Challenge section 1

XYZ Company wants its website to be fast for people everywhere, using Amazon CloudFront to share files like images. Your job is to make a storage bucket, add a file, make it public, set up CloudFront, create an error page, limit who can see the files, and test it all. Here’s how to do each step in very simple words:
Create an Public S3 Bucket with ACL Enabled: Upload any Image
Upload any Image
Create Custom Error Folder: create error.html and save it. Create block.html and save it.
create error.html and save it.
Create block.html and save it.
Make the Objects Publicturn off Block all public access,
turn off Block all public access,
Create a Amazon CloudFront Distribution: Create a cloudfront distribution with the S3 bucket
Create a cloudfront distribution with the S3 bucket
Configure Custom Error Page: Create custom error page with 404 http error,set error.html as the page,, andsave.
Create custom error page with 404 http error,
set error.html as the page,, andsave.
Restrict the Geographic Distribution of Your Content:Restrict the geographic region by blocking the country you are inSet block.html as the page, and save.
Restrict the geographic region by blocking the country you are in
Set block.html as the page, and save.
Make sure it all works. In the lab console, click Validate to check if CloudFront and S3 are set up correctly.
Sign out of the AWS Management Console.
In the lab console, click End Challenge and wait for it to stop.
You’ve finished the CloudFront challenge!
